puzzle.title: The Treasured Soul Who Questioned Authority
puzzle.intro: In the annals of the Quran, there lived a figure whose wealth and knowledge became legendary. This soul emerged from the earth with ornaments and riches that dazzled onlookers, yet their heart grew arrogant with pride. Join us as we unravel the mystery of one of the Quran's most cautionary tales, where material grandeur meets a humbling end beneath the very ground that birthed their fortune.
clues: I emerged from the earth adorned with ornaments that captured everyone's gaze.
I possessed both vast riches and deep knowledge that set me apart from others.
When a messenger came calling people to Allah, I questioned their authority with pride.
Those around me whispered that Allah's favor was not the source of my wealth.
My people marveled at my grandeur and wished for a fate similar to mine.
My magnificent structures were swallowed whole into the earth as divine punishment.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Shu'aib
Qarun (Korah)
The wealthy companion of Prophet Musa
Bal'am ibn Ba'ura
Zul-Qarnain's minister

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Building the golden calf for worship
Arrogantly questioning a prophet's authority
Hoarding wealth from a famine-stricken nation
Refusing to share knowledge with scholars
Asking a prophet for visual proof of resurrection

location.label: Key Element
options: The city of Midian
Palaces submerged in the sea
Ornaments and treasures
The burning bush of Mount Sinai
The sandy plains of ancient Egypt

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Transformed into a pillar of salt
Cast into the sea amid thunder and waves
Swallowed by the earth with all possessions
Raised alive to the heavens as a sign
Devoured by the earth with a lasting regret
outcome.answer: Swallowed by the earth with all possessions

puzzle.arabic: وَقَـٰرُونَ وَفِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ سَـٰبِقِينَ
puzzle.verse: And [We destroyed] Qārūn and Pharaoh and Hāmān. And Moses had already come to them with clear evidences, and they were arrogant in the land, but they were not outrunners [of Our punishment]. (29:39)
